fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 72 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(active) == limit` the function incorrectly returns `True` (allowed) instead of `False` (denied).

constraints.intent: At the branch condition, the comparison should trigger denial when len(active) >= limit, not only when strictly greater.
In the else branch (allowed path), the number of active timestamps must be strictly less than the limit; equality should have been caught by the if-branch.
Before using active__1 in the limit comparison, verify that the boundary case (equal to limit) will be handled as a denial, which the current strict-greater check misses.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and len(active__1) >= limit)

constraints.intent: The function must never return allowed=True when the number of active timestamps meets or exceeds the limit.
